classification_rationale: This is a corporate marketing and educational presentation about ConMed's Low Impact Laparoscopy technology (AirSeal® System and MicroLap® instruments). The video contains narration-free slides with marketing content, clinical study data, meta-analyses, product comparisons, and recovery benefit information. No actual FLS task performance is demonstrated—no trainer box, no surgical simulation, no time-based task execution, and no individual task rubric elements (peg transfer, pattern cutting, endoloop deployment, or suture knot tying). This is product/technology educational content, not an FLS MISTELS skill assessment.

demonstrated_techniques: Low-pressure pneumoperitoneum benefits in laparoscopic surgery
AirSeal® technology for maintaining pneumoperitoneum at reduced pressure
MicroLap® instruments with ultra-small openings (5mm equivalent with trocar compatibility)
Clinical evidence supporting reduced postoperative pain, shoulder pain, and analgesic use
Recovery metrics including PACU time reduction, morphine equivalents reduction, and hospital length of stay
common_errors_addressed: Challenges of low-pressure adoption: longer operative time and need to increase pressure during procedures
Comparison of standard insufflator versus AirSeal® system demonstrates superior surgical field maintenance at low pressure
content_focus: Clinical outcomes meta-analysis: 22 studies, 1263 patients showing pain reduction at 6, 12, 24 hours post-op
Current studies on AirSeal® at low pressure with physiologic benefits (25% airway pressure reduction, 10% CO2 reduction, 11% blood pressure reduction)
Recovery benefits: 25% PACU time reduction, 55% morphine reduction, 50% length of stay reduction

cannot_score_reason: Video is instructional/marketing content with no FLS task performance. Scoring rubric does not apply. No trainee or surgeon performing an actual FLS MISTELS task is visible in any frame.
